{{Quote|We're going to have to hit them where it hurts. Even if its only one little bit at a time.|Magir Paca|<ref name="GH" />}}
'''Magir Paca''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] as a leader of the [[Committee of Seven for Garosian Unification|underground resistance]] on [[Garos IV]].

==Biography==
Magir Paca was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Garos IV]].<ref name="GH" /> His [[Magir Paca's father|father]] was good friends with [[Tork Winger]],<ref name="TP">{{JournalCite|5|Turning Point (Charlene Newcomb)}}</ref> and Paca grew up calling him "Uncle".<ref name="GH" /> When Paca began his governmental service, it was as an aide to Winger, who was serving as the [[Assistant Minister of Defense]]. For over ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] Paca served the government with Winger as his mentor. By [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]], Paca held the post of [[Assistant Minister of Commerce]]. Unlike his mentor, who saw the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a positive force in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Paca despised the Empire and saw that it was a threat to Garos IV. He was one of the original leaders of the Garosian resistance, known as the [[Committee of Seven for Garosian Unification]]. The resistance worked to end the [[Garosian civil war]] between the native [[Garosian]]s and the [[Sundari/Legends|Sundari]] colonists by convincing them that the Empire was the true enemy.<ref name="TP" />

Paca, along with [[Desto Mayda]] and Sundari radical [[Camron Gelorik]], convinced a young [[Dair Haslip]] to join the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] as a [[Spy/Legends|spy]]. Haslip, who had been enthusiastic about joining the Empire until seeing his friend—and Desto Mayda's son—[[Jos Mayda|Jos]] killed by Imperial [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]], agreed.<ref name="TP" />

Paca's governmental position allowed him to covertly supply useful information and intelligence to the resistance. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Imperial forces realized there was a intelligence leak and set a trap to catch the perpetrator. Evidence against Paca was compiled, and the Empire suspected he was a member of the resistance and built a case against him. Fortunately for Paca, Governor Winger's adopted daughter, [[Alexandra Winger|Alexandra]] had [[Slicer/Legends|sliced]] into the Imperial [[Computer/Legends|computer]] files and discovered the information pertaining to Paca. Alexandra warned him of the danger, and Paca managed to disappear before Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] could arrest him. Going underground, Paca took a more active role in the resistance, and began to coordinate the underground's activities.<ref name="GH" />
